meaning: adj.涓庢按閬撴祴閲忔湁鍏崇殑,涓庢按鏂囧湴鐞嗘湁鍏崇殑
examples: The inshore hydrographic surveys were the basis of coastal and harbor charts.
Using this method, military hydrographic surveys are not a threat of use of force.
Good agreement between computation and measurement at two hydrographic stations is obtained.
The UK Hydrographic Office (UKHO) has issued a statement declining to comment on whether its charts were being used.
The ship will undergo a short maintenance period at Devonport before returning to her ocean deep survey hydrographic duties.
